name: Starter Battery
purpose: Starts the engine and supplies base-vehicle systems.
expected: About 12.6 V rested when healthy. Charging voltage rises with engine running.
fuses: Vehicle main fuse protection. Confirm exact Fiat fuse allocation from chassis documentation.
upgrade: Do not connect large habitation loads directly to the starter battery.
normalReadings: About 12.6 V rested when healthy. Charging voltage rises with engine running.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Leisure Battery
location: Habitation battery compartment. Confirm exact final position in vehicle record.
purpose: Supplies habitation lighting, pump, controls and appliance electronics.
expected: Lead-acid resting voltage near 12.6 V when fully charged. Interpret readings only after surface charge settles.
fuses: Main battery fuse plus downstream Calira distribution protection.
tests: Check charging voltage with engine running
upgrade: Planned LiFePO4 conversion requires charger, alternator-charging and low-temperature review.
normalReadings: Lead-acid resting voltage near 12.6 V when fully charged. Interpret readings only after surface charge settles.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Calira EVS 30/20-DS/IU
location: Electrical compartment. Confirmed by vehicle photograph.
purpose: Main habitation charger and electrical supply unit.
expected: Receives 230 V on hook-up and manages charging and habitation power distribution.
fuses: Inspect upstream mains protection, battery fuse and downstream distribution.
tests: Measure battery voltage before and after hook-up
upgrade: Retain unless testing proves failure. Integrate upgrades around it where safe.
normalReadings: Receives 230 V on hook-up and manages charging and habitation power distribution.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Calira VB06-1
purpose: Main fused 12 V distribution for refrigerator, battery monitor, permanent supply, pump and habitation circuits.
expected: Battery voltage should be present at the input. Each intact fuse should have voltage on both test points.
fuses: Refrigerator 20 A, battery monitor 7.5 A, permanent supply 15 A, pump 10 A, circuit 1 15 A, circuit 2 15 A.
tests: Test both sides of each fuse
upgrade: Add labels only after tracing the final loads for circuit 1 and circuit 2.
normalReadings: Battery voltage should be present at the input. Each intact fuse should have voltage on both test points.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Calira VB04
purpose: Auxiliary fused supply and signal distribution including heating, D+ and ignition-switched circuits.
expected: D+ should become active with the engine running. +15 should follow ignition state.
fuses: Dauer 15 A, Heizung 15 A, SML 10 A, D+ 5 A, +15 5 A.
tests: Test D+ with engine off and running
Test +15 with ignition off and on
upgrade: Do not add loads to D+ or +15 without relay isolation and current assessment.
normalReadings: D+ should become active with the engine running. +15 should follow ignition state.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: 230 V Hook-up and RCD
location: External inlet and internal consumer unit.
purpose: Supplies campsite mains power and charger input.
expected: Correct polarity, earth continuity and RCD operation are essential.
fuses: Protected by campsite supply, motorhome RCD and MCBs.
upgrade: Use a correctly rated outdoor hook-up lead. Avoid improvised adapters.
normalReadings: Correct polarity, earth continuity and RCD operation are essential.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Anker Solix C1000 Gen 2
purpose: Independent portable AC and DC power source.
expected: Operate separately from the factory Calira system unless a professionally designed changeover system is installed.
fuses: Internal protection within the Anker. External loads must still use suitable leads and ratings.
tests: Test AC output with a small load
upgrade: Do not back-feed the motorhome hook-up inlet.
normalReadings: Operate separately from the factory Calira system unless a professionally designed changeover system is installed.
replacement: Confirm exact replacement specification before ordering.

name: Alternator
location: Engine bay, driven by the auxiliary belt.
purpose: Charges the starter battery and supplies the vehicle while the engine runs.
normalReadings: Starter battery voltage should rise after starting
Compare alternator output and battery voltage under load
expected: Charging voltage should rise above rested battery voltage with the engine running.
fuses: Base-vehicle high-current protection. Refer to Fiat documentation for exact allocation.
relay: D+ or engine-running signal controls habitation charging logic.
tests: Switch on headlights and blower, then recheck
replacement: Match output rating, pulley and connector type to the fitted unit.
upgrade: A DC-DC charger should draw from the charging system through correctly protected cabling.

name: 230 V Consumer Unit
location: Inside the habitation area. Exact position should be added to the vehicle map.
purpose: Distributes mains power through RCD and MCB protection.
normalReadings: 230 V at incoming supply when connected
RCD test button should trip the device
expected: RCD and MCB protection should remain stable with healthy wiring and appliances.
fuses: MCBs protect final circuits. The campsite bollard also protects the incoming supply.
relay: None normally required for basic hook-up distribution.
tests: Investigate repeated trips by isolating circuits
faults: Charger works but sockets do not
replacement: Use an enclosure and devices suitable for motorhome use and the installed circuit ratings.
upgrade: Any inverter changeover must prevent back-feed and neutral-earth errors.

name: Water Pump Circuit
location: VB06 pump fuse to pump and pressure-switch wiring.
purpose: Supplies the fresh-water pump when the control system and pressure switch demand flow.
normalReadings: Battery voltage at the VB06 pump fuse
Voltage at pump during demand
expected: Pump should run when a tap opens and stop after pressure builds.
relay: Depends on fitted control and pump arrangement.
tests: Check 10 A fuse on both test points
Open a tap and test pump voltage
Power pump briefly from a protected test supply only if competent
maintenance: Check for leaks and air ingress
replacement: Match pump pressure, flow rate, current draw and mounting.
upgrade: Do not fit a higher-current pump without checking cable and fuse suitability.

name: Refrigerator 12 V Circuit
location: VB06 refrigerator fuse, D+ control path and refrigerator wiring.
purpose: Provides driving-mode refrigerator heating and control power.
normalReadings: D+ active only with engine running
Protected supply at refrigerator when driving mode is commanded
expected: 12 V cooling is intended mainly while driving and depends on the engine-running signal.
fuses: VB06 Kühlschrank 20 A and VB04 D+ 5 A.
relay: A refrigerator or split-charge relay may be used in the fitted architecture.
tests: Check D+ changes with engine state
Measure voltage at refrigerator under load
faults: Works on gas but not while driving
replacement: Match relay and element ratings to the fitted refrigerator.
upgrade: Lithium and DC-DC work must preserve the refrigerator control strategy.

name: Heating Control Circuit
location: VB04 heating fuse to Truma control and heater electronics.
purpose: Supplies 12 V control power for heating and hot-water operation.
manufacturer: Calira distribution and fitted Truma system
model: Confirm heater model from appliance label
normalReadings: Battery voltage at VB04 heating fuse
Stable supply during ignition and fan operation
expected: The heater needs reliable 12 V control power even when heat is produced from gas.
tests: Check for voltage collapse during fan operation
faults: Ignition failure caused by low voltage
replacement: Confirm appliance model and wiring before replacing controls.
upgrade: Battery changes must maintain stable voltage and correct protection.

name: Interior Lighting Circuits
location: VB06 habitation circuits to switches and light fittings.
normalReadings: Near battery voltage at fitting with light on
Low voltage drop across switches and connectors
expected: Lights should remain stable without flicker or excessive voltage loss.
fuses: Likely VB06 Stromkreis 1 or Stromkreis 2. Final allocation requires tracing.
relay: Normally switched locally without a relay.
tests: Identify correct fuse by controlled isolation
replacement: Use suitable 12 V fittings and preserve cable protection.
upgrade: LED replacements should be compatible with the supply and radio environment.

name: Victron SmartShunt 500 A
location: Future installation close to the leisure battery in the negative return.
purpose: Measures battery current, state of charge and energy use.
normalReadings: All habitation negative current must pass through the shunt
Charged-voltage and tail-current settings must suit battery chemistry
expected: Accurate readings depend on correct wiring and configuration.
fuses: Small fused positive sense wire required according to manufacturer instructions.
tests: Compare load current with known appliance
replacement: Use correct current rating and terminal hardware.
upgrade: Recommended first electrical upgrade before changing battery chemistry.

name: Victron Orion XS
location: Future ventilated installation between starter and leisure systems.
purpose: Provides controlled DC-DC charging, especially for a future lithium battery.
model: Orion XS 12/12-50A or final selected model
normalReadings: Input follows starter system
Output follows configured charge profile
Current limited to design value
expected: Requires correctly sized cables, fuses, ventilation and engine-running control.
fuses: High-current fuse at both battery ends based on cable and equipment design.
relay: Engine-running detection or remote enable as configured.
replacement: Final model depends on alternator capacity, battery and cable route.
upgrade: Install only after completing the charging-system design.
